11: ## PRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from kb context)
12: 
13: **Query:** "extract completion backlog"
14: 
15: ### Constraints (MUST respect)
16: - Session idle ≠ agent complete
17:   - Reason: Agents legitimately go idle during normal operation (loading, thinking, tool execution)
18: - orch complete must verify SYNTHESIS.md exists and is not placeholder before closing issue
19:   - Reason: 70% of agents completed without synthesis in 24h chaos period
20: - D.E.K.N. 'Next:' field must be updated when marking Status: Complete
21:   - Reason: Prevents stale investigations that mislead future agents
22: - External integrations require manual smoke test before Phase: Complete
23:   - Reason: OAuth feature shipped with passing tests but failed real-world use. Tests couldn't cover actual OAuth flow with Anthropic.
24: - SSE busy->idle cannot detect true agent completion
25:   - Reason: Agents go idle during loading, thinking, waiting for tools - not just when done
26: - Complete agents before spawning more - maintain spawn:complete ratio
27:   - Reason: Dec 25 reflection: 201 issues created vs 12 closed in 24h. Backlog grew faster than completion. 29 stuck agents accumulated. Spawn discipline prevents debt accumulation.

29: ### Prior Decisions
30: - Add Leave it Better after self-review, before completion criteria
31:   - Reason: Natural position in workflow - self-review validates quality, Leave it Better captures knowledge, completion gates exit
32: - Registry updates must happen before beads close in orch complete
33:   - Reason: Prevents inconsistent state where beads shows closed but registry shows active
34: - Refactor shell-out functions for testability
35:   - Reason: Extracting command construction into Build*Command functions allows unit testing of logic without side effects
36: - Implement 3-tier guardrail system: preflight checks, completion gates, daily reconciliation
37:   - Reason: Post-mortem showed 115 commits in 24h with 7 missing guardrails enabling runaway automation
38: - Post-registry lifecycle uses 4 state sources: OpenCode sessions, tmux windows, beads issues, workspaces
39:   - Reason: Registry removed due to false positive completion detection; derived lookups replace central state
40: - Use inline lineage metadata for project extraction, not centralized registry
41:   - Reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; centralized registry creates fragile external dependency
42: - orch complete auto-closes tmux window after successful verification
43:   - Reason: Complete means done - window goes away, beads closes, workspace remains. Prevents phantom accumulation (41 windows today). Debugging escape hatch: don't complete until ready to close.
44: - kb-cli owns artifact templates (investigation, decision, guide, research)
45:   - Reason: Consolidation complete - skill templates/ directories removed, kb-cli hardcoded updated with D.E.K.N.
46: - Progressive disclosure for skill bloat
47:   - Reason: 89% of feature-impl spawns use only 2-3 phases. Extract detailed phase guidance to reference docs, keep core workflow inline. Proven pattern from 2025-11-21 instruction optimization. Reduces 1757→~500 lines.
48: - CompletionService bridges SSE completion detection and slot management for headless agents

59: - Completion Verification Architecture
60:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/completion-verification.md
61:   - Summary:
62:     Completion verification operates through **three independent gates** (Phase, Evidence, Approval) that check different aspects of "done". Phase gate verifies agent claims completion, Evidence gate requires visual/test proof in beads comments, Approval gate (UI changes only) requires human sign-off. Verification is **tier-aware**: light tier checks Phase + commits, full tier adds SYNTHESIS.md, orchestrator tier checks SESSION_HANDOFF.md instead. The **5-tier escalation model** surfaces knowledge-producing work (investigation/architect/research) for mandatory orchestrator review before auto-closing. Cross-project detection uses SPAWN_CONTEXT.md to determine which directory to verify in. **Targeted bypasses** (`--skip-{gate} "reason"`) replace blanket `--force`, allowing specific gates to be skipped while others still run.
63:     
64:     ---
65:   - Why This Fails:
66:     ### 1. Evidence Gate False Positive
67:     
68:     
69:     **What happens:** Agent passes Evidence gate without actual visual verification.
70:     
71:     **Root cause:** Agent generates screenshot placeholder text ("Screenshot attached") without actually attaching screenshot. Evidence gate searches for keyword "screenshot", finds it, passes.
72:     
73:     **Why detection is hard:** Text-based keyword matching can't distinguish placeholder from actual proof.
74:     
75:     **Fix:** Approval gate for UI changes. Even if Evidence passes, human must verify via --approve.
76:     
77:     **Why this matters:** False positive on Evidence gate means broken UI ships thinking it's verified.
78:     
79:     ### 2. Approval Gate Bypass
80:     
81:     **What happens:** Non-UI changes accidentally avoid approval gate.
82:     
83:     **Root cause:** File path detection (`modifiedWebFiles()`) misclassifies files. `web-utils/` not under `web/`, approval skipped.
84:     
85:     **Why detection is hard:** File structure varies across projects. Heuristics (path contains "web") can miss edge cases.
86:     
87:     **Fix:** Explicit skill-based detection. `feature-impl` with UI flag requires approval, regardless of file paths.
88:     
89:     **Future:** Skill manifest declares "requires_ui_approval: true".
90:     
91:     ### 3. Cross-Project Verification Wrong Directory
92:     
93:     **What happens:** Verification runs in wrong directory, checks wrong tests, reports false failure.
94:     
95:     **Root cause:** `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` missing PROJECT_DIR, fallback uses workspace location (orch-go), but agent worked in orch-cli.
96:     
97:     **Why detection is hard:** Workspace location != work location. No guaranteed signal of where work happened.
98:     
99:     **Fix:** `orch spawn --workdir` explicitly sets PROJECT_DIR in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md. Verification reads it.
100:     
101:     **Prevention:** Make --workdir mandatory for cross-project spawns, fail spawn if missing.
102:

117: - Agent Completion Lifecycle
118:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/completion-lifecycle.md
119:   - Summary:
120:     The agent completion lifecycle is the transition from **Active Work** to **Knowledge Persistence**. It is the primary gate for the **Verification Bottleneck**. A healthy lifecycle ensures that agent findings are externalized (D.E.K.N.), workspaces are archived, and OpenCode sessions are purged to prevent "Registry Noise." The system uses a **Phase-based status model** where `Phase: Complete` in Beads is the only authoritative signal for success.

131: - Agent Lifecycle State Model
132:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/agent-lifecycle-state-model.md
133:   - Summary:
134:     Agent state exists across **four independent layers** (tmux windows, OpenCode in-memory, OpenCode on-disk, beads comments). These layers fall into two distinct categories: **state layers** (beads, workspace files) that represent what work was done, and **infrastructure layers** (OpenCode sessions, tmux windows) that represent transient execution resources. The dashboard reconciles these via a **Priority Cascade**: check beads issue status first (highest authority), then Phase comments, then registry state, then session existence. Status can appear "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual layer - this is a measurement artifact from combining multiple sources of truth.
135:     
136:     ---
137:   - Critical Invariants:
138:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
139:     2. **Beads issue closed = canonical completion** - All status queries defer to beads
140:     3. **Session existence ≠ agent still working** - Sessions persist indefinitely
141:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
142:     5. **Multiple sources must be reconciled** - No single source has complete truth
143:     6. **Tmux windows are UI layer only** - Not authoritative for state
144:     
145:     ---
146:   - Why This Fails:
147:     ### Failure Mode 1: Dashboard Shows "Active" When Agent is Done
148:     
149:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows agent as active, but `bd show <id>` says status=closed
150:     
151:     **Root cause:** Dashboard caching or SSE lag - hasn't received beads update yet
152:     
153:     **Why it happens:**
154:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
155:     - `orch complete` closes beads issue
156:     - Beads issue status = closed
157:     - Dashboard hasn't refreshed or polled beads yet
158:     - Dashboard still shows cached "active" state
159:     
160:     **Fix:** Refresh dashboard browser tab (forces beads query)
161:     
162:     **NOT the fix:** Deleting OpenCode session (treats symptom, not cause)
163:     
164:     ### Failure Mode 2: "Dead" Agents That Actually Completed
165:     
166:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
167:     
168:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happened before dashboard queried, cascade reached session check
169:     
170:     **Why it happens:**
171:     - Agent completed, beads issue closed
172:     - Session cleanup ran (manual or automatic)
173:     - Dashboard cascade: beads check → no issue (closed) → session check → no session → "dead"
174:     
175:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts beads/Phase check before session existence check
176:     
177:     ### Failure Mode 3: Agent Went Idle But Not Complete
178:     
179:     **Symptom:** Session status is "idle" but no `Phase: Complete` comment
180:     
181:     **Root cause:** Agent ran out of context, crashed, or didn't follow completion protocol
182:     
183:     **Why it happens:**
184:     - Session exhausts context (150k tokens)
185:     - Agent stops responding
186:     - SSE event: `session.status = idle`
187:     - No `Phase: Complete` was ever written
188:     - Dashboard shows "idle" or "waiting"
189:     
190:     **This is expected behavior.** Session idle ≠ work complete. Only agents that explicitly run `bd comment <id> "Phase: Complete"` are considered done.
191:     
192:     **Fix:** Check workspace for what agent accomplished, then either:
193:     - `orch complete <id> --force` if work is done
194:     - `orch abandon <id>` if work is incomplete
195:     
196:     ### Failure Mode 4: Cross-Project Agents Not Visible
197:     
198:     **Symptom:** Agent spawned with `--workdir /other/project` doesn't appear in dashboard
199:     
200:     **Root cause:** Dashboard only scans current project's `.orch/workspace/` directory
201:     
202:     **Why it happens:**
203:     - Workspace created in `/other/project/.orch/workspace/`
204:     - Dashboard running from `orch-go` only sees `orch-go/.orch/workspace/`
205:     - Cross-project discovery requires querying OpenCode sessions for unique directories
206:     
207:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Multi-project workspace cache built from OpenCode session directories
208:

214: - Orchestrator Session Lifecycle
215:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md
216:   - Summary:
217:     Orchestrator sessions operate in a **three-tier hierarchy** (meta-orchestrator → orchestrator → worker) where each level is completed by the level above. Orchestrators produce **SESSION_HANDOFF.md** (not SYNTHESIS.md) and **wait** for completion (not /exit). They track via **session registry** (not beads) because orchestrators manage conversations, not work items. Frame collapse occurs when orchestrators drop levels and do work below their station - detected externally, not self-diagnosed. Checkpoint discipline uses duration thresholds (2h/3h/4h) as a proxy for context exhaustion.
218:     
219:     ---
220:   - Why This Fails:
221:     ### 1. Frame Collapse (Orchestrator → Worker)
222:     
223:     **What happens:** Orchestrator drops into worker-level implementation (editing code, debugging, investigating).
224:     
225:     **Root cause:** Vague goals → exploration mode → investigation → debugging. **Framing cues override skill instructions.**
226:     
227:     **Why detection is hard:** Orchestrators can't self-diagnose frame collapse. The frame defines what's visible, so from inside the collapsed frame, the behavior feels appropriate.
228:     
229:     **Detection signals:**
230:     - Edit tool usage on code files (not orchestration artifacts)
231:     - Time spent >15 minutes on direct fixes
232:     - SESSION_HANDOFF.md shows "Manual fixes" sections
233:     - Post-mortem reveals work that should have been spawned
234:     
235:     **NOT the fix:** Adding more ABSOLUTE DELEGATION RULE warnings. The agent already knows. The problem is framing, not awareness.
236:     
237:     **Prevention:**
238:     1. Provide specific goals with action verbs, concrete deliverables, success criteria
239:     2. Use WHICH vs HOW test: meta decides WHICH focus, orchestrator decides HOW to execute
240:     3. Frame collapse check in SESSION_HANDOFF.md template
241:     4. Potential: OpenCode plugin tracking Edit usage on code vs artifacts
242:     
243:     **Trigger pattern:** Failure-to-implementation. After agents fail, orchestrator tries to "just fix it" instead of trying different spawn strategy.
244:     
245:     ### 2. Self-Termination Attempts
246:     
247:     **What happens:** Spawned orchestrator tries to run `orch session end` or `/exit` instead of waiting for completion.
248:     
249:     **Root cause:** ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template contradicted the hierarchical completion model (told orchestrator to self-terminate).
250:     
251:     **Why it's wrong:** Breaks the "completed by level above" invariant. Orchestrator can't verify its own work from meta perspective.
252:     
253:     **Fix:** Template updated Jan 2026 to instruct "write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT".
254:     
255:     ### 3. Session Registry Drift
256:     
257:     **What happens:** `~/.orch/sessions.json` shows status "active" for completed sessions.
258:     
259:     **Root cause:** `orch complete` didn't update session registry status (only closed beads issues or removed registry entries).
260:     
261:     **Why it matters:** Stale active sessions accumulate, `orch status` shows ghost sessions, registry becomes unreliable.
262:     
263:     **Fix:** `orch complete` now updates status to "completed", `orch abandon` updates to "abandoned" - sessions preserved for history, not removed.
264:

267: - Dashboard Agent Status Calculation
268:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/dashboard-agent-status.md
269:   - Summary:
270:     The dashboard calculates agent status through a **Priority Cascade model**: check Phase first (highest priority), then registry state, then session existence. Status can be "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual check - this is a measurement artifact, not a system failure. The root issue was observation infrastructure (missing events, double-counting metrics, state not surfaced), not broken completion logic.
271:     
272:     ---
273:   - Critical Invariants:
274:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
275:     2. **Registry is source of truth for abandonment** - Human judgment, can't be inferred
276:     3. **Session may outlive completion** - Session existence ≠ agent still working
277:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
278:     
279:     ---
280:   - Why This Fails:
281:     ### Failure Mode 1: "Dead" Agents That Completed
282:     
283:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
284:     
285:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happens async, dashboard checks session existence as fallback
286:     
287:     **Why it happens:**
288:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
289:     - `orch complete` verifies and closes beads issue
290:     - Session cleanup happens later (or not at all)
291:     - Dashboard cascade reaches session check → sees no session → "dead"
292:     
293:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts Phase check before session check
294:     
295:     ### Failure Mode 2: Metrics Show Wrong Completion Rate
296:     
297:     **Symptom:** `orch stats` showed 72% completion when reality was 89%
298:     
299:     **Root cause:** Metrics counted events (double-counting) instead of deduplicating entities
300:     
301:     **Why it happens:**
302:     - `agent.completed` event emitted by both `orch complete` AND beads close hook
303:     - Metrics counted events → some completions counted 2x
304:     - Result: inflated total, deflated completion %
305:     
306:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Metrics deduplicate by beads_id before calculating ratios
307:     
308:     ### Failure Mode 3: Work Completed via Bypass Paths
309:     
310:     **Symptom:** Beads issue closed but no completion event, dashboard doesn't update
311:     
312:     **Root cause:** `bd close` (direct) doesn't emit events, only `orch complete` does
313:     
314:     **Why it happens:**
315:     - Multiple paths to completion: `orch complete`, `bd close`, `bd sync` with commit message
316:     - Only `orch complete` emits events
317:     - Other paths are invisible to observation infrastructure
318:     
319:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Beads close hook emits `agent.completed` event

323: - Daemon Autonomous Operation
324:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/daemon-autonomous-operation.md
325:   - Summary:
326:     The daemon is an **autonomous agent spawner** that operates in a **poll-spawn-complete cycle**: polls beads for `triage:ready` issues, infers skill from issue type, spawns within capacity limits, monitors for `Phase: Complete`, verifies and closes. The daemon operates **independently of orchestrators** - orchestrators triage (label issues ready), daemon spawns (batch processing), orchestrators synthesize (review completed work). Skill inference uses **issue type** (not labels) to map task→investigation, bug→systematic-debugging, etc. Capacity management uses **WorkerPool** with reconciliation against OpenCode to free stale slots.
327:     
328:     ---
329:   - Why This Fails:
330:     ### 1. Capacity Starvation
331:     
332:     **What happens:** Pool shows MaxAgents active, but `orch status` shows fewer actual agents running.
333:     
334:     **Root cause:** Spawn failures don't release slots. Agent spawned, counted against pool, but spawn fails (bad skill name, missing context, etc.) - slot never released.
335:     
336:     **Why detection is hard:** Pool only knows about attempts, not outcomes. No feedback loop from spawn failure to pool.
337:     
338:     **Fix:** Reconciliation with OpenCode. Query actual sessions, release slots for non-existent agents.
339:     
340:     **Prevention:** Spawn tracking with retry limits (`pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`).
341:     
342:     ### 2. Duplicate Spawns
343:     
344:     **What happens:** Same issue spawned multiple times by daemon on consecutive polls.
345:     
346:     **Root cause:** Spawn latency. Issue labeled `triage:ready` at poll N, daemon spawns, but spawn hasn't transitioned issue to `in_progress` by poll N+1. Daemon sees same issue still ready, spawns again.
347:     
348:     **Why detection is hard:** Race condition between poll interval (60s) and spawn transition time (variable).
349:     
350:     **Fix:** Spawn deduplication via tracking. Track spawned beads IDs in memory, skip on subsequent polls until status confirms transition.
351:     
352:     **Source:** `pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`
353:     
354:     ### 3. Skill Inference Mismatch
355:     
356:     **What happens:** Daemon spawns wrong skill for issue type.
357:     
358:     **Root cause:** Issue type doesn't match actual work needed. User creates `task` for implementation work (should be `feature`), daemon infers `investigation`.
359:     
360:     **Why detection is hard:** Type is set at creation, can't be changed by daemon. Daemon trusts type.
361:     
362:     **Fix:** Manual override. Orchestrator updates issue type before labeling `triage:ready`, or spawns manually with correct skill.
363:     
364:     **Prevention:** Better issue creation prompts, type validation, skill override via labels (future).
365:

441: 	// Track agents by title to deduplicate (OpenCode can have multiple sessions with same title)
442: 	// Keep the most recently updated session for each title
443: 	seenTitles := make(map[string]int) // title -> index in agents slice
444: 
445: 	for _, s := range sessions {
446: 		createdAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Created/1000, 0)
447: 		updatedAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
448: 		runtime := now.Sub(createdAt)
449: 		timeSinceUpdate := now.Sub(updatedAt)
450: 
451: 		// Determine status based on recent activity
452: 		// Priority: dead (3min silence) > active (recent) > idle (10min+)
453: 		// Dead agents need attention - they're crashed/stuck/killed.
454: 		status := "active"
455: 		if timeSinceUpdate > deadThreshold {
456: 			status = "dead" // No activity for 3+ minutes = dead (crashed/stuck/killed)
457: 		} else if timeSinceUpdate > activeThreshold {
458: 			status = "idle" // Session exists but hasn't had recent activity
459: 		}
460: 
461: 		// NOTE: IsProcessing is now populated client-side via SSE session.status events.
462: 		// Previously we called client.IsSessionProcessing(s.ID) here, but that makes
463: 		// an HTTP call per session which caused 125% CPU when dashboard polled frequently.
464: 		// The frontend already receives busy/idle state from OpenCode SSE and updates
465: 		// is_processing in real-time, so we don't need to fetch it here.
466: 
467: 		agent := AgentAPIResponse{
468: 			ID:           s.Title,
469: 			SessionID:    s.ID,
470: 			Status:       status,
471: 			Runtime:      formatDuration(runtime),
472: 			SpawnedAt:    createdAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
473: 			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
474: 			IsProcessing: false,       // Populated client-side via SSE
475: 			ProjectDir:   s.Directory, // Set from session directory for project filtering
476: 		}
477: 
478: 		// Derive beadsID and skill from session title
479: 		if s.Title != "" {
480: 			agent.BeadsID = extractBeadsIDFromTitle(s.Title)
481: 			agent.Skill = extractSkillFromTitle(s.Title)
482: 			agent.Project = extractProjectFromBeadsID(agent.BeadsID)
483: 
484: 		}
485: 
486: 		// Only include sessions that were spawned via orch spawn (have beads ID)
487: 		// This filters out interactive/ad-hoc OpenCode sessions
488: 		if agent.BeadsID == "" {
489: 			continue
490: 		}
491: 
492: 		// OPTIMIZATION: Mark sessions older than beadsFetchThreshold as stale.
493: 		// We still include them in the response (for dashboard visibility) but skip
494: 		// beads data fetch for performance. With 600+ sessions but only ~6-10 active,
495: 		// fetching beads for all would require 400+ RPC calls = 3+ seconds per request.
496: 		// By marking old sessions as stale and skipping beads fetch, we reduce to ~20-50 RPC calls.
497: 		isStale := timeSinceUpdate > beadsFetchThreshold
498: 		if isStale {
499: 			agent.IsStale = true
500: 			agent.Status = "idle" // Stale agents are not actively running
501: 		}
502: 
503: 		// Track if this agent should be filtered after Phase check using two-threshold logic
504: 		// Don't filter yet - we need to check beads Phase: Complete first
505: 		// Don't filter stale agents - they're already marked with is_stale=true
506: 		// Use aggressive threshold for marking (agents idle > 4h are candidates for filtering)
507: 		if status == "idle" && timeSinceUpdate > displayThreshold && !isStale {
508: 			pendingFilterByBeadsID[agent.BeadsID] = true
509: 		}
510: 
511: 		// For cross-project agent visibility: use cached PROJECT_DIR from workspace
512: 		// This is O(1) lookup and should happen for ALL agents (including stale)
513: 		// to ensure correct project filtering when using --workdir spawns.
514: 		// The session's directory may be wrong (orchestrator's cwd vs target workdir).
515: 		if agent.BeadsID != "" {
516: 			if agentProjectDir := wsCache.lookupProjectDir(agent.BeadsID); agentProjectDir != "" {
517: 				beadsProjectDirs[agent.BeadsID] = agentProjectDir
518: 			}
519: 		}
520: 
521: 		// Collect beads ID for batch fetch - only for NON-STALE agents with beads ID.
522: 		// Stale agents (older than beadsFetchThreshold) are included in response but
523: 		// skip beads fetch for performance optimization.
524: 		// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md
525: 		if agent.BeadsID != "" && !seenBeadsIDs[agent.BeadsID] && !isStale {
526: 			beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, agent.BeadsID)
527: 			seenBeadsIDs[agent.BeadsID] = true
528: 		}
529: 
530: 		// Deduplicate by title - keep the most recently updated session
531: 		// OpenCode can have multiple sessions with the same title (e.g., resumed agents)
532: 		if existingIdx, exists := seenTitles[s.Title]; exists {
533: 			// Compare updated_at to keep the more recent session
534: 			existingUpdatedAt, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, agents[existingIdx].UpdatedAt)
535: 			if updatedAt.After(existingUpdatedAt) {
536: 				// Replace the existing agent with this newer one
537: 				agents[existingIdx] = agent
538: 			}
539: 			// Skip appending since we either replaced or kept the existing one
540: 			continue
541: 		}
542: 
543: 		seenTitles[s.Title] = len(agents)
544: 		agents = append(agents, agent)
545: 	}
546: 
547: 	// Add tmux-only agents
548: 	workersSessions, _ := tmux.ListWorkersSessions()
549: 	for _, sessionName := range workersSessions {
550: 		windows, _ := tmux.ListWindows(sessionName)
551: 		for _, win := range windows {
552: 			if win.Name == "servers" || win.Name == "zsh" {
553: 				continue
554: 			}
555: 
556: 			beadsID := extractBeadsIDFromWindowName(win.Name)
557: 			skill := extractSkillFromWindowName(win.Name)
558: 			project := extractProjectFromBeadsID(beadsID)
559: 
560: 			// Check if already in agents list
561: 			alreadyIn := false
562: 			for _, a := range agents {
563: 				if (beadsID != "" && a.BeadsID == beadsID) || (a.ID != "" && strings.Contains(win.Name, a.ID)) {
564: 					alreadyIn = true
565: 					break
566: 				}
567: 			}
568: 
569: 			if !alreadyIn {
570: 				agents = append(agents, AgentAPIResponse{
571: 					ID:      win.Name,
572: 					BeadsID: beadsID,
573: 					Skill:   skill,
574: 					Project: project,
575: 					Status:  "active",
576: 					Window:  win.Target,
577: 				})
578: 
579: 				// Collect beads ID for batch fetch
580: 				if beadsID != "" && !seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] {
581: 					beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsID)
582: 					seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] = true
583: 
584: 					// For cross-project agent visibility: use cached PROJECT_DIR
585: 					// This replaces expensive directory scanning with O(1) lookup
586: 					if agentProjectDir := wsCache.lookupProjectDir(beadsID); agentProjectDir != "" {
587: 						beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = agentProjectDir
588: 					}
589: 				}
590: 			}
591: 		}
592: 	}
593: 
594: 	// Add completed workspaces (those with SYNTHESIS.md or light-tier completions)
595: 	// Reuse cached workspace entries to avoid redundant directory reads
596: 	// Multi-project support: entries may come from different project workspace directories
597: 	if len(wsCache.workspaceEntries) > 0 {
598: 		for _, entry := range wsCache.workspaceEntries {
599: 			if !entry.IsDir() {
600: 				continue
601: 			}
602: 
603: 			// Check if already in active list
604: 			// Active session IDs have format "workspace [beads-id]", workspace names don't
605: 			alreadyIn := false
606: 			workspaceName := entry.Name()
607: 			for _, a := range agents {
608: 				if a.ID == workspaceName || strings.HasPrefix(a.ID, workspaceName+" ") {
609: 					alreadyIn = true
610: 					break
611: 				}
612: 			}
613: 
614: 			if alreadyIn {
615: 				continue
616: 			}
617: 
618: 			// Use the lookup method for multi-project support
619: 			workspacePath := wsCache.lookupWorkspacePathByEntry(entry.Name())
620: 			synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
621: 			hasSynthesis := false
622: 
623: 			// Check if SYNTHESIS.md exists (indicates full-tier completion)
624: 			if _, err := os.Stat(synthesisPath); err == nil {
625: 				hasSynthesis = true
626: 			}
627: 
628: 			// Only add workspaces that have SYNTHESIS.md for now
629: 			// Light-tier completions will be detected via Phase: Complete in beads comments
630: 			if !hasSynthesis {
631: 				// For light-tier, check if there's a SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (indicates it's a valid spawn)
632: 				spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
633: 				if _, err := os.Stat(spawnContextPath); err != nil {
634: 					continue // Not a valid spawn workspace
635: 				}
636: 			}
637: 
638: 			agent := AgentAPIResponse{
639: 				ID:     entry.Name(),
640: 				Status: "completed",
641: 			}
642: 
643: 			// Set updated_at from workspace name date suffix or file modification time
644: 			// This ensures proper sorting in archive section
645: 			if parsedDate := extractDateFromWorkspaceName(entry.Name()); !parsedDate.IsZero() {
646: 				agent.UpdatedAt = parsedDate.Format(time.RFC3339)
647: 			} else if hasSynthesis {
648: 				// Fallback to file modification time of SYNTHESIS.md
649: 				if info, err := os.Stat(synthesisPath); err == nil {
650: 					agent.UpdatedAt = info.ModTime().Format(time.RFC3339)
651: 				}
652: 			} else {
653: 				// For light-tier, use SPAWN_CONTEXT.md modification time
654: 				spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
655: 				if info, err := os.Stat(spawnContextPath); err == nil {
656: 					agent.UpdatedAt = info.ModTime().Format(time.RFC3339)
657: 				}
658: 			}
659: 
660: 			// Read session ID from workspace
661: 			if sessionID := spawn.ReadSessionID(workspacePath); sessionID != "" {
662: 				agent.SessionID = sessionID
663: 			}
664: 
665: 			// Read spawn time from workspace
666: 			if spawnTime := spawn.ReadSpawnTime(workspacePath); !spawnTime.IsZero() {
667: 				agent.SpawnedAt = spawnTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
668: 			}
669: 
670: 			// Parse synthesis (only for full-tier)
671: 			if hasSynthesis {
672: 				if synthesis, err := verify.ParseSynthesis(workspacePath); err == nil {
673: 					agent.Synthesis = &SynthesisResponse{
674: 						TLDR:           synthesis.TLDR,
675: 						Outcome:        synthesis.Outcome,
676: 						Recommendation: synthesis.Recommendation,
677: 						DeltaSummary:   summarizeDelta(synthesis.Delta),
678: 						NextActions:    synthesis.NextActions,
679: 					}
680: 				}
681: 				// Read raw SYNTHESIS.md content for inline rendering in dashboard
682: 				synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
683: 				if content, err := os.ReadFile(synthesisPath); err == nil {
684: 					agent.SynthesisContent = string(content)
685: 				}
686: 			}
687: 
688: 			// Extract beadsID from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (more reliable than parsing name)
689: 			agent.BeadsID = extractBeadsIDFromWorkspace(workspacePath)
690: 			// Fallback to extracting from workspace name if SPAWN_CONTEXT.md doesn't have it
691: 			if agent.BeadsID == "" {
692: 				agent.BeadsID = extractBeadsIDFromTitle(entry.Name())
693: 			}
694: 			agent.Skill = extractSkillFromTitle(entry.Name())
695: 
696: 			// NOTE: We intentionally DON'T extract PROJECT_DIR or fetch beads data for completed workspaces.
697: 			// Completed workspaces have already done their work - they don't need phase updates.
698: 			// The close_reason is nice-to-have but not worth spawning bd processes.
699: 			// This optimization prevents CPU spikes from fetching beads data for 600+ historical workspaces.
700: 
701: 			agents = append(agents, agent)
702: 		}
703: 	}
704: 
705: 	// Batch fetch beads data (phase from comments, task from issues, close_reason for completed)
706: 	// This is the same pattern used by orch status for efficiency.
707: 	// Uses TTL cache to prevent CPU spikes from spawning 20+ bd processes per request.
708: 	if len(beadsIDsToFetch) > 0 {
709: 		// Fetch all open issues in one call (cached with TTL)
710: 		openIssues, _ := globalBeadsCache.getOpenIssues()
711: 
712: 		// Batch fetch all issues (including closed) for close_reason (cached with TTL)
713: 		// Uses bd show which works for any issue status
714: 		allIssues, _ := globalBeadsCache.getAllIssues(beadsIDsToFetch)
715: 
716: 		// Batch fetch comments for all beads IDs (cached with TTL)
717: 		// Use project-aware batch fetch for cross-project agent visibility
718: 		commentsMap := globalBeadsCache.getComments(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsProjectDirs)
719: 
720: 		// Build investigation directory cache ONCE before the agent loop.
721: 		// This prevents O(n²) behavior: without this, discoverInvestigationPath would call
722: 		// os.ReadDir() 2-3 times per agent, scanning 500+ files each time.
723: 		// With 300+ agents, that's 300 × 500 × 2 = 300,000+ file comparisons.
724: 		// The cache reduces this to a single ReadDir() call per directory.
725: 		uniqueProjectDirs := make([]string, 0, len(beadsProjectDirs))
726: 		seenDirs := make(map[string]bool)
727: 		for _, dir := range beadsProjectDirs {
728: 			if dir != "" && !seenDirs[dir] {
729: 				seenDirs[dir] = true
730: 				uniqueProjectDirs = append(uniqueProjectDirs, dir)
731: 			}
732: 		}
733: 		invDirCache := buildInvestigationDirCache(uniqueProjectDirs)
734: 
735: 		// Populate phase, task, close_reason, and status for each agent using Priority Cascade model.
736: 		// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md for design.
737: 		for i := range agents {
738: 			if agents[i].BeadsID == "" {
739: 				continue
740: 			}
741: 
742: 			// Get task from open issue title first
743: 			if issue, ok := openIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
744: 				agents[i].Task = truncate(issue.Title, 60)
745: 			}
746: 
747: 			// If not in open issues, try all issues (for closed ones)
748: 			if agents[i].Task == "" {
749: 				if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
750: 					agents[i].Task = truncate(issue.Title, 60)
751: 					// For completed agents without synthesis, use close_reason as fallback
752: 					if agents[i].Synthesis == nil && issue.CloseReason != "" {
753: 						agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
754: 					}
755: 				}
756: 			}
757: 
758: 			// Gather completion signals for Priority Cascade model
759: 			issueClosed := false
760: 			phaseComplete := false
761: 
762: 			// Check if beads issue is closed (Priority 1)
763: 			if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
764: 				issueClosed = strings.EqualFold(issue.Status, "closed")
765: 				// Capture close_reason if available
766: 				if issueClosed && issue.CloseReason != "" && agents[i].CloseReason == "" {
767: 					agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
768: 				}
769: 			}
770: 
771: 			// Get phase from comments (Priority 2)
772: 			if comments, ok := commentsMap[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
773: 				phaseStatus := verify.ParsePhaseFromComments(comments)
774: 				if phaseStatus.Found {
775: 					agents[i].Phase = phaseStatus.Phase
776: 					phaseComplete = strings.EqualFold(phaseStatus.Phase, "Complete")
777: 
778: 					// Stalled detection: if active agent has same phase for 15+ minutes
779: 					// Advisory only - surfaces in Needs Attention but doesn't auto-abandon.
780: 					// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-design-stalled-agent-detection-agents.md
781: 					if agents[i].Status == "active" && phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt != nil {
782: 						timeSincePhase := now.Sub(*phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt)
783: 						if timeSincePhase > stalledThreshold {
784: 							agents[i].IsStalled = true
785: 						}
786: 					}
787: 				}
788: 				// Extract investigation_path from comments for investigation tab rendering
789: 				if investigationPath := verify.ParseInvestigationPathFromComments(comments); investigationPath != "" {
790: 					agents[i].InvestigationPath = investigationPath
791: 				}
792: 			}
793: 
794: 			// Populate project_dir from beadsProjectDirs lookup (for workspace path construction)
795: 			// Track if we got a reliable project dir from workspace cache (vs using session directory)
796: 			hasReliableProjectDir := false
797: 			if agentProjectDir, ok := beadsProjectDirs[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
798: 				agents[i].ProjectDir = agentProjectDir
799: 				hasReliableProjectDir = true
800: 			}
801: 
802: 			// Auto-discover investigation path if not provided via beads comment
803: 			// This uses a fallback chain: .kb/investigations/ matching -> workspace .md files
804: 			// Uses invDirCache to avoid O(n²) directory scanning (built once before this loop)
805: 			// IMPORTANT: Only auto-discover if we have a reliable project dir from workspace cache.
806: 			// The session directory (s.Directory) may be the orchestrator's cwd due to OpenCode --attach bug,
807: 			// which would cause us to search the wrong project's .kb/investigations/ for cross-project agents.
808: 			if agents[i].InvestigationPath == "" && hasReliableProjectDir {
809: 				workspaceName := agents[i].ID
810: 				if idx := strings.Index(workspaceName, " ["); idx != -1 {
811: 					workspaceName = workspaceName[:idx]
812: 				}
813: 				discoveredPath := discoverInvestigationPath(workspaceName, agents[i].BeadsID, agents[i].ProjectDir, invDirCache)
814: 				if discoveredPath != "" {
815: 					agents[i].InvestigationPath = discoveredPath
816: 				}
817: 			}
818: 
819: 			// Read investigation file content for inline rendering in dashboard
820: 			if agents[i].InvestigationPath != "" {
821: 				if content, err := os.ReadFile(agents[i].InvestigationPath); err == nil {
822: 					agents[i].InvestigationContent = string(content)
823: 				}
824: 			}
825: 
826: 			// Get workspace path for SYNTHESIS.md check (Priority 3)
827: 			workspacePath := wsCache.lookupWorkspace(agents[i].BeadsID)
828: 			// Fallback: For untracked agents, try looking up by workspace name from session title
829: 			if workspacePath == "" && agents[i].ID != "" {
830: 				workspaceName := agents[i].ID
831: 				if idx := strings.Index(workspaceName, " ["); idx != -1 {
832: 					workspaceName = workspaceName[:idx]
833: 				}
834: 				workspacePath = wsCache.lookupWorkspacePathByEntry(workspaceName)
835: 			}
836: 
837: 			// Use Priority Cascade to determine final status
838: 			// Priority order: issueClosed > phaseComplete > SYNTHESIS.md > sessionStatus
839: 			agents[i].Status = determineAgentStatus(issueClosed, phaseComplete, workspacePath, agents[i].Status)
840: 
841: 			// For completed agents, also check close_reason if synthesis is null
842: 			if agents[i].Status == "completed" && agents[i].Synthesis == nil && agents[i].CloseReason == "" {
843: 				if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok && issue.CloseReason != "" {
844: 					agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
845: 				}
846: 			}
847: 
848: 			// Read synthesis content for active agents that have a workspace with SYNTHESIS.md
849: 			// (Completed workspaces already have this populated above in the workspace scan)
850: 			if workspacePath != "" && agents[i].SynthesisContent == "" {
851: 				synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
852: 				if content, err := os.ReadFile(synthesisPath); err == nil {
853: 					agents[i].SynthesisContent = string(content)
854: 					// Also parse synthesis if not already parsed
855: 					if agents[i].Synthesis == nil {
856: 						if synthesis, err := verify.ParseSynthesis(workspacePath); err == nil {
857: 							agents[i].Synthesis = &SynthesisResponse{
858: 								TLDR:           synthesis.TLDR,
859: 								Outcome:        synthesis.Outcome,
860: 								Recommendation: synthesis.Recommendation,
861: 								DeltaSummary:   summarizeDelta(synthesis.Delta),
862: 								NextActions:    synthesis.NextActions,
863: 							}
864: 						}
865: 					}
866: 				}
867: 			}
868: 		}
869: 
870: 		// Fetch gap analysis from spawn events for each agent
871: 		gapAnalysisMap := getGapAnalysisFromEvents(beadsIDsToFetch)
872: 		for i := range agents {
873: 			if agents[i].BeadsID == "" {
874: 				continue
875: 			}
876: 			if gapData, ok := gapAnalysisMap[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
877: 				agents[i].GapAnalysis = gapData
878: 			}
879: 		}
880: 
881: 		// Post-Phase filtering: remove agents using two-threshold ghost filtering
882: 		// Uses IsVisibleByDefault to determine if agent should be shown
883: 		// This ensures Phase: Complete agents are always visible
884: 		filtered := make([]AgentAPIResponse, 0, len(agents))
885: 		for _, agentItem := range agents {
886: 			// Determine status for filtering ("running" vs "idle")
887: 			status := agentItem.Status
888: 			if status != "active" && status != "idle" && status != "completed" {
889: 				// Map other statuses to idle for filtering purposes
890: 				status = "idle"
891: 			} else if status == "active" {
892: 				status = "running"
893: 			}
894: 
895: 			// Get last activity time
896: 			lastActivity := time.Time{}
897: 			if agentItem.LastActivityAt != "" {
898: 				lastActivity, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, agentItem.LastActivityAt)
899: 			}
900: 
901: 			// Apply IsVisibleByDefault filter only if agent was marked as pending
902: 			if pendingFilterByBeadsID[agentItem.BeadsID] {
903: 				if !agent.IsVisibleByDefault(status, lastActivity, agentItem.Phase) {
904: 					continue // Ghost agent - filter out
905: 				}
906: 			}
907: 
908: 			filtered = append(filtered, agentItem)
909: 		}
910: 		agents = filtered
911: 	}
912: 
913: 	// NOTE: The duplicate SYNTHESIS.md check that was here has been removed.
914: 	// All agents are now included in beadsIDsToFetch, so the Priority Cascade
915: 	// in determineAgentStatus() handles all status determination in one place.
916: 	// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md
917: 
918: 	// Fetch token usage and last activity for agents with valid session IDs
919: 	// Parallelized to avoid sequential HTTP calls causing ~20s delays with 200+ agents.
920: 	// Uses goroutines with semaphore to limit concurrent requests.
921: 	// Both tokens and activity are extracted from the same GetMessages call for efficiency.
922: 	type sessionResult struct {
923: 		index    int
924: 		tokens   *opencode.TokenStats
925: 		activity *opencode.LastActivity
926: 	}
927: 	resultChan := make(chan sessionResult, len(agents))
928: 
929: 	// Limit concurrent HTTP requests to avoid overwhelming the OpenCode server
930: 	const maxConcurrent = 20
931: 	sem := make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrent)
932: 
933: 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
934: 	for i := range agents {
935: 		// Skip agents without session ID, completed agents, or idle agents.

1080: // determineAgentStatus implements the Priority Cascade model for agent status.
1081: // This is the single source of truth for determining agent status.
1082: //
1083: // Priority order (highest to lowest):
1084: //  1. Beads issue closed → "completed" (orchestrator verified completion)
1085: //  2. Phase: Complete reported AND session dead → "awaiting-cleanup" (agent done, needs orch complete)
1086: //  3. Phase: Complete reported → "completed" (agent declared done, still has active session)
1087: //  4. SYNTHESIS.md exists AND session dead → "awaiting-cleanup" (artifact proves completion, needs cleanup)
1088: //  5. SYNTHESIS.md exists → "completed" (artifact proves completion)
1089: //  6. Session activity → sessionStatus ("active", "idle", or "dead")
1090: //
1091: // The "awaiting-cleanup" status distinguishes completed-but-orphaned agents from crashed agents.
1092: // This helps orchestrators prioritize: awaiting-cleanup needs orch complete, dead needs investigation.
1093: // See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md for design rationale.
1094: // See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-handle-multiple-agents-same-beads.md for awaiting-cleanup addition.
1095: func determineAgentStatus(issueClosed bool, phaseComplete bool, workspacePath string, sessionStatus string) string {
1096: 	// Priority 1: Beads issue closed → completed (orchestrator verified completion)
1097: 	if issueClosed {
1098: 		return "completed"
1099: 	}
1100: 
1101: 	hasSynthesis := checkWorkspaceSynthesis(workspacePath)
1102: 	isDead := sessionStatus == "dead"
1103: 
1104: 	// Priority 2: Phase: Complete reported AND session dead → awaiting-cleanup
1105: 	// Agent finished work and reported completion, but orchestrator hasn't run orch complete.
1106: 	// This is NOT an error state - the agent did its job, just needs cleanup.
1107: 	if phaseComplete && isDead {
1108: 		return "awaiting-cleanup"
1109: 	}
1110: 
1111: 	// Priority 3: Phase: Complete reported (session still active/idle) → completed
1112: 	if phaseComplete {
1113: 		return "completed"
1114: 	}
1115: 
1116: 	// Priority 4: SYNTHESIS.md exists AND session dead → awaiting-cleanup
1117: 	// Agent wrote synthesis artifact (proof of completion) but session is dead.
1118: 	// Similar to Phase: Complete case - needs cleanup, not investigation.
1119: 	if hasSynthesis && isDead {
1120: 		return "awaiting-cleanup"
1121: 	}
1122: 
1123: 	// Priority 5: SYNTHESIS.md exists (session still active/idle) → completed
1124: 	if hasSynthesis {
1125: 		return "completed"
1126: 	}
1127: 
1128: 	// Priority 6: Session activity (fallback)
1129: 	// "dead" agents without completion signals truly need attention (crashed/stuck)
1130: 	return sessionStatus
1131: }

3: **Date:** 2026-02-14

11: ## Problem Statement
12: 
13: The coaching plugin (1831 lines, `plugins/coaching.ts`) tracks 8 orchestrator metrics. Real-world data (1022 metrics collected) shows noise:
14: - `action_ratio` (381 events) and `analysis_paralysis` (355 events) dominate but generate false positives (workers legitimately read more than they write, bash sequences are common)
15: - `compensation_pattern` (79 events) fires frequently on keyword overlap without being actionable
16: - `context_ratio` (41 events) is a ratio that doesn't drive behavior change
17: - `frame_collapse` (13 events) and `circular_pattern` (7 events) are high-signal, low-noise
18: - `behavioral_variation` (145 events) has value but needs tuning
19: - `context_usage` (1 event) is the only worker metric recorded so far
20: 
21: Additionally, `orch stats` provides operational metrics (spawns, completions, verification) but no behavioral health visibility. And there's no mechanism to auto-surface system health when an orchestrator session starts.
22: 
23: ### Success Criteria
24: 
25: 1. Orchestrator metrics: 4 high-signal metrics (down from 8)
26: 2. Stats integration: `orch stats` shows coaching summary section
27: 3. Session start: Orchestrator automatically sees last-24h health summary
28: 4. No regressions in worker health tracking
29: 
30: ### Constraints
31: 
32: - Plugins cannot see LLM response text (fundamental OpenCode constraint)
33: - Plugin hooks available: `tool.execute.after`, `event` (for `session.created`), `experimental.chat.messages.transform`, `config`

40: ## Fork 1: How to Detect the 3 New Orchestrator Metrics

The infrastructure extracts keywords from the task description, runs tiered search (local → global), and injects results into SPAWN_CONTEXT.md. The orchestrator skill's instruction to manually run `kb context` before every spawn is a holdover from before this automation existed.
47: 
48: **Action:** Remove manual `kb context` instruction from orchestrator skill. The real quality gate is the automated context score shown in spawn output (e.g., "Context: ✓ 90/100 (excellent)"). If context is low, the orchestrator should improve the task description, not manually run `kb context`.

54: ### Fork 1b: `completion_backlog`
55: 
56: **What it detects:** Agents at Phase:Complete for >10 minutes without `orch complete` being run.
57: 
58: **Options:**
59: 
60: - **A: Plugin-side detection via tool calls.** Plugin monitors for bash commands containing `bd show` or `orch status` and parses output for Phase:Complete agents. Fragile — depends on orchestrator running specific commands.
61: - **B: Plugin-side detection via periodic polling.** Plugin periodically shells out to check agent status. Violates the "plugins observe, don't act" pattern and adds latency.
62: - **C: Go-side detection in `orch stats` or `orch serve`.** The Go backend already has agent status logic in `serve_agents.go`. Add a check there and expose via API. Plugin reads from API when checking behavioral health.
63: - **D: Plugin `event` hook + session metadata.** Listen to `session.status` events for idle sessions, cross-reference with workspace Phase files.
64: 
65: **Substrate says:**
66: - Model: "Plugins cannot see LLM response text" — but they CAN see tool call outputs. However, plugins shouldn't be parsing `bd show` output.
67: - Model: "Observation coupled to intervention" is already a known anti-pattern.
68: - Principle: Compose over monolith — the Go backend already knows agent status.
69: 
70: **RECOMMENDATION:** Option C — Go-side detection. Add `completion_backlog` detection to `orch serve` (specifically the `/api/agents` handler or a new `/api/coaching/health` endpoint). The Go backend already reads workspace Phase comments and knows agent timestamps. Write a metric to `coaching-metrics.jsonl` from the Go side, making it available to both `orch stats` and the dashboard.
71: 
72: This is architecturally cleaner: the Go backend has the data, the plugin has the detection pattern. Don't force the plugin to discover what the Go backend already knows.
73: 
74: **Implementation:**
75: ```go
76: // In serve_agents.go or a new coaching health check:
77: // When serving /api/agents, check for agents where:
78: // 1. Phase == "Complete" (from workspace Phase comment)
79: // 2. completedAt + 10min < now
80: // Write metric to coaching-metrics.jsonl
81: ```
82: 
83: **Trade-off:** This breaks the "plugin is the only metric writer" pattern. But the alternative (plugin polling agent status) is worse — it creates a circular dependency where the plugin calls the API that calls the plugin.
84: 
85: **Open question for orchestrator:** Is the Go backend writing to coaching-metrics.jsonl acceptable, or should completion_backlog live in a separate metrics file? Recommend same file for unified aggregation.

Re-reading the requirements: `direct_implementation` is Edit/Write outside .kb/.orch/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. `frame_collapse` is orchestrator editing CODE files. These are the same thing. **Recommend treating `direct_implementation` as a refinement of `frame_collapse` rather than a separate metric.** Update the allowlist on `frame_collapse` and keep the name.

178: ## Fork 4: Session-Start Auto-Surfacing Mechanism
179: 
180: **Decision:** How to automatically show health summary when orchestrator session starts.
181: 
182: **Options:**
183: 
184: - **A: Plugin `event` hook — listen for `session.created`.** When a session is created, inject a health summary message via `client.session.prompt({ noReply: true })`.
185: - **B: Plugin `config` hook.** Inject health summary into `config.instructions` at plugin initialization. Problem: runs once at server start, not per-session.
186: - **C: First-tool-call detection in `tool.execute.after`.** When a new session's first tool call arrives, inject health summary. Uses existing hook infrastructure.
187: - **D: OpenCode `experimental.chat.system.transform` hook.** Inject health summary into the system prompt for each session. Problem: fires on every LLM call, not just session start.
188: - **E: External mechanism — orchestrator skill instruction.** Add "run `orch stats --days 1` at session start" to orchestrator skill. No plugin changes needed.
189: 
190: **Substrate says:**
191: - Model: "Observation coupled to intervention" is a known anti-pattern. But this is a one-time injection, not continuous.
192: - The Explore agent confirmed `session.created` event IS available via the `event` hook.
193: - Plugin already has access to `client.session.prompt()` for injection.
194: - Constraint: `session.created` event fires for ALL sessions (workers too), not just orchestrator sessions.
195: 
196: **RECOMMENDATION:** Option A — listen for `session.created` via the `event` hook. When fired:
197: 
198: 1. Check if session is a worker (via metadata) — if so, skip.
199: 2. Read last 24h of coaching-metrics.jsonl (shared reader from Fork 3's `pkg/coaching/`).
200: 3. Generate a compact health summary.
201: 4. Inject via `client.session.prompt({ sessionID, noReply: true })`.
202: 
203: **Implementation sketch:**
204: ```typescript
205: event: async ({ event }) => {
206:   if (event.type === "session.created") {
207:     const sessionInfo = event.properties?.info;
208:     const sessionId = sessionInfo?.id;
209:     if (!sessionId) return;
210: 
211:     // Skip workers (check metadata.role)
212:     if (sessionInfo?.metadata?.role === 'worker') return;
213: 
214:     // Read recent metrics and generate summary
215:     const summary = generateHealthSummary();
216:     if (summary) {
217:       await client.session.prompt({
218:         sessionID: sessionId,
219:         prompt: summary,
220:         noReply: true,
221:       });
222:     }
223:   }
224: }
225: ```
226: 
227: **Critical consideration:** The health summary should include:
228: - Last 24h coaching metric counts by type
229: - Completion backlog (agents at Phase:Complete)
230: - Agent spawn/completion rates (from events.jsonl or `orch stats --days 1 --json`)
231: 
232: **Problem:** Plugin reads coaching-metrics.jsonl directly (TypeScript). But `orch stats` reads events.jsonl (Go). For the session-start summary to include operational stats, either:
233: 1. Plugin shells out to `orch stats --days 1 --json` and parses the output
234: 2. Plugin only shows coaching metrics, operational stats come separately
235: 3. Plugin calls the `orch serve` API endpoint for combined data
236: 
237: **Recommend Option 1** — shell out to `orch stats --days 1 --json`. The plugin already reads files; shelling out to orch stats gets all the data in one call. Use child_process.execSync or similar.
238: 
239: **Trade-off:** Shell-out adds ~500ms latency to session start. Acceptable for a one-time injection.
240: 
241: **Fallback (Option E):** If the plugin approach is too complex or fragile, the simplest fallback is adding an instruction to the orchestrator skill: "At session start, run `orch stats --days 1` to review system health." This requires zero code changes but relies on the orchestrator following the instruction.

11: // IsActiveForConcurrency determines if an agent should count toward the concurrency limit.

27: func IsActiveForConcurrency(status string, lastActivity time.Time, phase string) bool {
28: 	now := time.Now()
29: 	oneHour := 1 * time.Hour
30: 
31: 	// Running agents always count

44: 	}

46: 	return false
47: }
